title: Object Detection Project Scope
concept: Defining the goal: detecting specific objects (like cars or pedestrians) with bounding boxes.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of an object detection project?

options.text: To identify specific objects in images
To change the color of objects

exercises.explain: The main purpose of an object detection project is to identify specific objects in images.

cards.title: What is Object Detection?
cards.body: Object detection involves locating and classifying objects present in images. For instance, we can find cars or people in a photo.

cards.title: Defining the Goal
cards.body: The goal of an object detection project is to create a system that can detect specific items, like cars or pedestrians, and show their locations.

cards.title: Finding Objects with a Map
cards.body: Imagine using a treasure map. The map helps you find treasures; similarly, our object detection system helps find specific items in images!

cards.title: Example of Object Detection
cards.body: In an image with both cars and trees, our system will create boxes around each car to highlight them.

cards.title: Common Misconception
cards.body: Some think object detection just recognizes an object. It actually finds both the object and its location in the image!

cards.title: Object Detection Visualization
cards.body: Notice the difference in detection using bounding boxes.

left.text: Car with a bounding box around it!

exercises.prompt: What do bounding boxes do in object detection?

options.text: They show where an object is located in an image

exercises.explain: Bounding boxes indicate the precise location of detected objects in an image, highlighting them.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Object detection only identifies what's in an image but doesn’t show where it is.
exercises.explain: Object detection not only identifies objects but also provides their locations using bounding boxes.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: The goal of object detection is to find specific objects in an image and show their ________.
exercises.sentence: The goal of object detection is to find specific objects in an image and show their bounding boxes.
exercises.explain: We show 'bounding boxes' around detected objects to indicate their positions.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes object detection?

options.text: It only recognizes objects without knowing their location.
It identifies objects and locates them with bounding boxes.
It enhances the quality of images by applying filters.

exercises.explain: The correct statement describes object detection as identifying objects and showing where they are using bounding boxes.

exercises.prompt: In an object detection project, which is an essential feature?

options.text: Displaying specific objects using bounding boxes

exercises.explain: Displaying specific objects with bounding boxes is crucial because it allows users to see not just what is in the photo, but exactly where each item is located!
